title: Using a Grill Mat to Cook Delicate Shrimp Skewers
meta_description: Discover how using a grill mat for shrimp skewers prevents sticking and flare-ups. Get juicy results every time with easy cleanup and expert tips.
html:
There is nothing quite like the disappointment of watching a plump, juicy shrimp slip through the grates of your grill and vanish into the charcoal abyss. Aside from the loss of dinner, shrimp are notorious for sticking to metal bars and drying out in seconds. If you love seafood but hate the cleanup and the gamble of grilling shellfish, you are not alone. This is where finding the best grill mat for shrimp skewers changes everything, turning a high-stakes cooking session into a stress-free experience.

The Battle Against the Grates

Shrimp are structurally difficult to grill. They are small, they cook incredibly fast, and their flesh is tender. Unlike a steak or a burger, which forms a crust and holds together, shrimp can become mushy or stick aggressively to hot iron. When you try to pry them off, you often leave the tasty shell behind or tear the meat apart.

Furthermore, shrimp usually require a marinade or a brushing of oil and butter to taste their best. If you apply this directly to the grates, it drips down, hits the burners or coals, and causes sudden flare-ups. These flash fires singe the shell before the meat is cooked through, leaving you with a bitter, charred taste. A non-stick barrier solves these physics problems instantly.

Why a Grill Mat for Shrimp Skewers is a Game Changer

Using a specialized mat creates a flat, non-porous surface between your food and the flame. This does three specific things for your seafood. First, it guarantees that your marinade stays in contact with the shrimp rather than dripping away, which amps up the flavor. Second, it provides a stable surface so you can turn the skewers without the shrimp spinning or breaking. Finally, it allows for even heat distribution, meaning you get that perfect pink opaque color without rubbery, overcooked edges.

For this task, the BBQ Grill Mats are an excellent choice. These mats allow you to grill delicate foods without flare-ups or sticking, and because they are reusable, they are an eco-friendly alternative to aluminum foil. They handle the high heat of a grill while maintaining a non-stick surface that is perfect for the sugars and acids often found in shrimp marinades.

Preparing Your Shrimp for the Mat

Before you fire up the grill, proper preparation is key. Even with a non-stick surface, you want to ensure your shrimp are ready to cook evenly.

  • Select the Right Size: Large or jumbo shrimp (16/20 count) are best for skewers because they are less likely to overcook before you get them off the heat.
  • Thaw Completely: If frozen, thaw them in the fridge overnight. Grilling frozen shrimp results in a steamed, rubbery texture.
  • Skewering Strategy: Use metal skewers for better heat conduction, or bamboo skewers soaked in water for 30 minutes. Thread the shrimp straight rather than curved to ensure they lay flat on the mat.
  • The Marinade: Keep it simple. Olive oil, garlic, lemon zest, and parsley work wonders. Avoid excessive sugar in the rub, as it can caramelize too quickly, even on a mat.

The Grilling Process: Step-by-Step

Once your shrimp are prepped and the grill is hot, follow these steps for perfect results. The beauty of using a mat is that you have far more control than you do with open grates.

  1. Preheat the Grill: Get your grill to a medium-high heat, around 350°F to 400°F. Place the BBQ Grill Mats on the grates to preheat them as well. This helps sear the shrimp immediately upon contact.
  2. Oil the Mat: While the mats are non-stick, a light coating of high-smoke-point oil (like avocado or canola) adds an extra layer of insurance and richness.
  3. Place the Skewers: Lay the shrimp skewers onto the mat. Make sure they are in a single layer and not touching each other, allowing the smoke and heat to circulate.
  4. Watch for the Flip: Shrimp cook fast—usually 2 to 3 minutes per side. You will see the underside turn pink and opaque. Use tongs to flip the skewers. Because they aren't stuck to the grates, they will release effortlessly.
  5. Finish and Serve: Once the shrimp are pink and curled slightly, remove them immediately. Do not leave them on the mat with the heat off, or they will continue cooking in the residual heat.

Cleaning and Maintenance

One of the best reasons to use a grill mat for shrimp skewers is the cleanup. Traditionally, cleaning shrimp grease off grates involves a wire brush and a lot of elbow grease. With a reusable mat, you simply remove it from the grill once it has cooled slightly.

Wash the mats in warm soapy water with a soft sponge or cloth. They are dishwasher safe, which makes the process even easier. Avoid using abrasive scouring pads or metal utensils directly on the surface, as these can scratch the coating and reduce its non-stick effectiveness over time. Once dry, they can be rolled up or stored flat until your next cookout.

FAQ

Can I use metal skewers directly on the grill mat?

Yes, metal skewers are perfectly fine to use on grill mats. Just be careful when placing them down to avoid puncturing the material if you press too hard, though the mats are generally quite durable.

Will I still get grill marks on my shrimp?

Using a mat will soften the definition of grill marks. You might get some light searing lines, but you won't get the deep charred stripes you get from direct grates. However, you gain better flavor retention and significantly less risk of the shrimp drying out.

Are grill mats safe for high temperatures?

Absolutely. Quality mats, like the BBQ Grill Mats, are designed to withstand typical grilling temperatures, usually up to 500°F. Just ensure you are not using them directly over a open flare-up flame, as direct contact with fire can damage the material.
